title: Inheritance Tree of Classes Should Not Be Too Deep
description: In object-oriented programming, inheritance is a way to categorize and reuse code by creating classes based on previously created ones. However, creating a deep inheritance tree can lead to complex and unmaintainable source code. This rule flags an issue when the inheritance tree, starting from Object, exceeds the allowed depth.
recommendation: Consider using 'composition' instead of 'inheritance' when facing a deep inheritance tree. It's also possible to adjust the depth limit according to the complexity of the project.
impact: Having a deep inheritance tree can lead to code that is difficult to maintain and understand. This can also introduce potential bugs as changes in a parent class can have unintended effects in a child class.

checks.message: Inheritance tree of classes should not be too deep. Consider using composition instead of inheritance.
